prompt: A corporate office wants to support WELL Mind mental health promotion. Which policy intervention is most aligned with reducing stigma and encouraging help-seeking?

options.text: Communicate leadership support for mental health resources and normalize use of EAP counseling
Prohibit discussion of stress or mental health during performance reviews
Require employees to disclose diagnoses before receiving any accommodations
Replace all wellness programming with mandatory overtime

explanation: WELL Mind emphasizes destigmatizing mental health and promoting access to support services such as Employee Assistance Programs through visible leadership endorsement and clear communication.

distractorExplanations.b: Silencing mental health discussion increases stigma and discourages help-seeking.
distractorExplanations.c: Mandatory diagnosis disclosure can violate privacy and deter people from seeking support.
distractorExplanations.d: Mandatory overtime undermines mental health and contradicts WELL Mind goals.

prompt: Which space design best supports WELL Mind stress recovery during the workday?

options.text: A dedicated restorative room with comfortable seating, adjustable lighting, and acoustic privacy
An open break area directly adjacent to a noisy copy center
A windowless storage closet labeled as a wellness zone
A high-traffic corridor with bench seating only

explanation: Restorative spaces under WELL Mind provide a calm, private environment where occupants can decompress with appropriate comfort, lighting, and sound control.

distractorExplanations.b: High noise levels prevent effective stress recovery.
distractorExplanations.c: Poor environmental quality in a storage space does not meet restorative design intent.
distractorExplanations.d: Corridor seating lacks privacy and calm needed for recovery.

prompt: A WELL v2 project provides occupant access to an on-site landscaped garden with seating. Which Mind concept does this primarily address?

options.text: Nature access
Thermal comfort
Water quality
Materials transparency

explanation: Accessible outdoor green spaces with places to rest support WELL Mind nature access, which links exposure to natural environments with stress reduction and cognitive restoration.

distractorExplanations.b: Thermal comfort addresses temperature and humidity, not biophilic access.
distractorExplanations.c: Water quality relates to hydration and contaminant control.
distractorExplanations.d: Materials transparency concerns product ingredient disclosure.

prompt: Which employer program best exemplifies WELL Mind altruism strategies?

options.text: Paid volunteer time off paired with organized community service opportunities
Elimination of all charitable giving policies
Penalties for employees who participate in community events
Restricting community engagement to executive staff only

explanation: WELL Mind altruism features encourage prosocial behavior through structured opportunities such as paid volunteer time, which research associates with improved well-being and purpose.

distractorExplanations.b: Removing charitable policies removes support for altruistic engagement.
distractorExplanations.c: Penalties actively discourage community participation.
distractorExplanations.d: Limiting engagement to executives excludes most occupants from altruism benefits.

prompt: At a building entrance, which design element best supports WELL Community universal design for wheelchair users?

options.text: A level or ramped entry with adequate clear width and maneuvering space at the door
Steps only at all public entrances with no alternative route
Heavy round doorknobs mounted above reachable height
Narrow 24-inch clear width at the primary entrance

explanation: Universal design under WELL Community requires equitable access, including step-free entries and sufficient clear width and maneuvering space for mobility device users.

distractorExplanations.b: Steps-only entries exclude wheelchair users from independent access.
distractorExplanations.c: Round knobs and unreachable hardware create operability barriers.
distractorExplanations.d: 24-inch clear width is insufficient for wheelchair passage at entrances.

prompt: A facility manager is developing a WELL Community emergency preparedness plan. What should be included first?

options.text: Documented procedures for evacuation, shelter-in-place, and occupant communication during emergencies
A policy that assumes all occupants will self-evacuate without guidance
Removal of all emergency signage to reduce visual clutter
Deferring all planning until after certification is awarded

explanation: WELL Community emergency preparedness requires proactive planning with clear protocols for different emergency types and reliable occupant notification systems.

distractorExplanations.b: Assuming self-evacuation without guidance fails vulnerable occupants and coordination needs.
distractorExplanations.c: Removing signage impairs emergency response.
distractorExplanations.d: Preparedness must be established during design and operations, not deferred.

prompt: Which strategy best supports WELL Community civic engagement in a multi-tenant office building?

options.text: Host regular forums where occupants can provide input on building policies and community initiatives
Make all building decisions without occupant communication
Restrict building amenities to a single tenant
Eliminate all shared common spaces

explanation: Civic engagement under WELL Community fosters participation in decisions affecting the shared environment, strengthening belonging and shared responsibility.

distractorExplanations.b: Excluding occupants from decisions undermines engagement.
distractorExplanations.c: Tenant-only amenities reduce community participation across the building population.
distractorExplanations.d: Removing common spaces eliminates venues for community interaction.

prompt: A hospital outpatient clinic serves diverse language communities. Which inclusive design approach best supports WELL Community goals?

options.text: Wayfinding and health information signage in primary languages spoken by the occupant population
English-only signage regardless of user demographics
Removal of all written signage to simplify graphics
Using decorative script fonts with low legibility throughout

explanation: Inclusive design in WELL Community addresses diverse user needs, including multilingual communication so occupants can navigate and access services equitably.

distractorExplanations.b: English-only signage creates barriers for non-English speakers.
distractorExplanations.c: Removing signage eliminates critical orientation information.
distractorExplanations.d: Low-legibility fonts hinder comprehension for many users.

prompt: Which wayfinding strategy best helps visitors with cognitive disabilities locate destinations in a large civic center?

options.text: Consistent color-coded paths with simple landmark references at decision points
Identical corridor finishes with no differentiation between wings
Complex alphanumeric codes without maps or icons
Signage placed only at ceiling height in small type

explanation: WELL Community wayfinding supports intuitive navigation through consistent visual cues, landmarks, and redundant information formats that reduce cognitive load.

distractorExplanations.b: Monotonous environments increase disorientation.
distractorExplanations.c: Codes alone without visual support are difficult for many users to interpret.
distractorExplanations.d: High, small signage is hard to read and misses users who need eye-level cues.

prompt: An employer offers mindfulness and resilience workshops during lunch hours. Which WELL concept area does this primarily support?

options.text: Mind — stress management
Water — hydration
Light — circadian lighting
Materials — VOC limits

explanation: Structured stress management programming, including mindfulness training, is a core WELL Mind strategy to help occupants build coping skills and reduce workplace stress.

distractorExplanations.b: Hydration programs belong to WELL Water.
distractorExplanations.c: Circadian lighting is a WELL Light strategy.
distractorExplanations.d: VOC limits are addressed under WELL Materials.

prompt: Which restroom design feature best reflects WELL Community inclusive design for users of all genders and abilities?

options.text: Single-user all-gender restrooms with accessible fixtures and clear signage
Restrooms accessible only by stairs on upper floors
Fixtures mounted above standard reach range with no accessible stall
No signage indicating restroom locations

explanation: Inclusive restrooms that are accessible, clearly signed, and usable by diverse gender identities support WELL Community goals for equitable, dignified facility access.

distractorExplanations.b: Stairs-only access excludes people with mobility limitations.
distractorExplanations.c: Unreachable fixtures and missing accessible stalls violate inclusive design.
distractorExplanations.d: Missing signage impairs wayfinding to essential facilities.

prompt: A project team wants to increase nature access for interior office workers on a high floor. Which strategy is most effective?

options.text: Provide views to landscaped outdoor areas and incorporate living plants in regularly occupied spaces
Install floor-to-ceiling mirrors with no vegetation or outdoor views
Paint all walls fluorescent orange to simulate sunlight
Block all windows with opaque film to reduce glare only

explanation: WELL Mind nature access can be achieved through direct views to nature and interior biophilic elements such as plants, which support restoration even when outdoor access is limited.

distractorExplanations.b: Mirrors do not provide biophilic or nature-connected benefits.
distractorExplanations.c: Wall color alone does not substitute for nature exposure.
distractorExplanations.d: Blocking all views eliminates a primary nature access opportunity.

prompt: During annual emergency preparedness drills, what is the primary WELL Community objective?

options.text: Ensure occupants understand procedures and can respond safely, including people with disabilities
Demonstrate that alarms can be disabled to avoid disruption
Confirm that only security staff need to know evacuation routes
Verify that all exits remain locked during drills

explanation: Emergency drills validate that plans are understood and inclusive, accounting for occupants who may need assistance or alternative egress strategies.

distractorExplanations.b: Disabling alarms undermines preparedness training.
distractorExplanations.c: All occupants should know relevant emergency procedures.
distractorExplanations.d: Locked exits during evacuation drills create unsafe conditions.

prompt: Which initiative best demonstrates altruism within a WELL-certified workplace?

options.text: A structured donation-matching program supporting employee-chosen charities
A ban on charitable activities during work hours
Requiring employees to donate a fixed percentage of salary without choice
Public recognition only for highest donors, excluding all other participation

explanation: WELL Mind altruism encourages voluntary prosocial giving; matching programs amplify employee-directed charitable engagement and foster a culture of contribution.

distractorExplanations.b: Banning charitable activity removes opportunities for altruistic engagement.
distractorExplanations.c: Mandatory giving without choice is coercive rather than supportive altruism.
distractorExplanations.d: Recognizing only top donors discourages broad participation.

prompt: A university building renovation must meet WELL Community universal design. Which corridor detail is most critical for mobility device users?

options.text: Minimum 36-inch clear passage width with passing spaces at intervals
28-inch clear width with frequent dead-end pinch points
Thick plush carpet that creates high rolling resistance throughout
Threshold changes greater than 1 inch at every doorway

explanation: Adequate corridor clear width and passing spaces are fundamental universal design requirements so wheelchair and scooter users can navigate independently.

distractorExplanations.b: 28-inch width is below accessible passage requirements.
distractorExplanations.c: High rolling resistance carpeting impedes wheelchair mobility.
distractorExplanations.d: Large thresholds create barriers at door transitions.

prompt: A property team installs tactile and Braille signage at elevators and floor directories. Which WELL Community concept does this primarily address?

options.text: Wayfinding
Nourishment
Thermal comfort
Air filtration

explanation: Multisensory signage including tactile and Braille elements supports wayfinding for people with visual impairments, a key inclusive navigation strategy in WELL Community.

distractorExplanations.b: Nourishment addresses healthy food environments.
distractorExplanations.c: Thermal comfort concerns temperature and humidity control.
distractorExplanations.d: Air filtration is part of WELL Air.

prompt: Which mental health promotion practice is most appropriate for WELL Mind in a large organization?

options.text: Training managers to recognize distress signs and refer employees to confidential resources
Publishing individual employee therapy notes on an internal portal
Disciplining employees who request mental health accommodations
Eliminating all break periods to maximize productivity

explanation: Manager training on mental health awareness and referral pathways supports early intervention while respecting confidentiality, aligning with WELL Mind promotion strategies.

distractorExplanations.b: Publishing therapy notes violates privacy and increases stigma.
distractorExplanations.c: Penalizing accommodation requests discourages help-seeking.
distractorExplanations.d: Removing breaks increases stress and harms mental health.

prompt: A mixed-use development partners with a local nonprofit to host voter registration and neighborhood meetings on-site. Which WELL concept does this support?

options.text: Community — civic engagement
Mind — nature access
Water — moisture control
Sound — reverberation time

explanation: Hosting civic activities and partnerships with local organizations strengthens community connection and democratic participation, core WELL Community civic engagement goals.

distractorExplanations.b: Nature access concerns exposure to natural environments.
distractorExplanations.c: Moisture control is a building envelope or water management topic.
distractorExplanations.d: Reverberation time is an acoustic performance metric.

prompt: Which feature best supports stress recovery for healthcare workers between shifts?

options.text: A quiet decompression lounge separated from clinical alarms and foot traffic
A staff lounge located inside the emergency department trauma bay
Bright overhead fluorescent lighting at maximum output with no dimming
Shared seating in a public waiting area with patient announcements

explanation: Healthcare stress recovery spaces should buffer clinical stressors through acoustic separation, comfortable furnishings, and controllable lighting to allow genuine rest.

distractorExplanations.b: Locating lounges in active trauma areas prevents recovery.
distractorExplanations.c: Harsh constant lighting is not restorative.
distractorExplanations.d: Public waiting areas lack privacy and calm for staff decompression.

prompt: An emergency preparedness kit for a WELL project office should include which item set?

options.text: First aid supplies, flashlights, water, contact lists, and backup communication tools
Only decorative plants and marketing brochures
Confidential HR files and spare office furniture
Non-perishable food exclusively, with no medical or communication items

explanation: WELL Community emergency preparedness kits should support basic medical needs, illumination, hydration, and communication when normal systems are disrupted.

distractorExplanations.b: Decorative items do not aid emergency response.
distractorExplanations.c: HR files and furniture are not emergency response supplies.
distractorExplanations.d: Food alone omits critical first aid and communication resources.

prompt: Which interior finish choice best supports inclusive design for occupants with low vision?

options.text: High visual contrast between walls, floors, doors, and signage
Low-contrast beige-on-cream surfaces throughout
Highly reflective glossy floors that create glare
Random patterned carpet with no logical pathway cues

explanation: Visual contrast helps people with low vision distinguish architectural elements and hazards, supporting WELL Community inclusive and universal design principles.

distractorExplanations.b: Low contrast makes edges and openings difficult to perceive.
distractorExplanations.c: Glare from reflective floors impairs vision for many users.
distractorExplanations.d: Confusing patterns without cues can increase disorientation.

prompt: A tech campus creates a walking trail through native plantings accessible from the workplace. What dual WELL benefit is most significant?

options.text: Supports Mind nature access and provides a restorative break from indoor work
Eliminates all WELL Water testing requirements
Replaces emergency preparedness planning
Guarantees WELL Platinum without documentation

explanation: Outdoor walking paths through natural landscaping increase nature exposure and offer a physical and psychological break, advancing WELL Mind restoration goals.

distractorExplanations.b: Nature trails do not waive Water feature compliance.
distractorExplanations.c: Emergency planning remains a separate Community requirement.
distractorExplanations.d: Certification requires documented compliance across all concepts.

prompt: Which hearing-accessibility feature supports universal design in a conference center?

options.text: Hearing loop (induction loop) systems in assembly spaces
Carpet-only flooring with no acoustic treatment
Open ceilings that maximize HVAC noise in meeting rooms
Audio announcements only, with no visual paging

explanation: Hearing loops transmit audio directly to compatible hearing aids and cochlear implants, a recognized universal design strategy for equitable access to spoken information.

distractorExplanations.b: Carpet alone does not assist hearing aid users with speech intelligibility.
distractorExplanations.c: Excessive background noise degrades communication for everyone.
distractorExplanations.d: Audio-only paging excludes people who are Deaf or hard of hearing.

prompt: A building uses a consistent icon system and floor numbering at eye level near elevators. Which WELL Community strategy does this implement?

options.text: Wayfinding with redundant, legible cues at decision points
Emergency generator fuel storage
VOC emission testing for furniture
Circadian melanopic lux targeting

explanation: Consistent icons and floor identifiers at decision points reduce navigation errors and support users with diverse abilities, aligning with WELL Community wayfinding design.

distractorExplanations.b: Generator fuel storage is mechanical infrastructure, not wayfinding.
distractorExplanations.c: VOC testing belongs to WELL Materials.
distractorExplanations.d: Melanopic lux is a WELL Light circadian metric.

prompt: An organization implements flexible work policies and discourages after-hours email expectations. Which WELL Mind outcome is most directly supported?

options.text: Reduced chronic stress and improved work-life balance
Increased indoor particulate matter
Higher MERV filter requirements
Mandatory nature immersion for all staff daily

explanation: Policies that protect personal time and reduce always-on work culture are stress management strategies under WELL Mind that help prevent burnout.

distractorExplanations.b: Particulate matter relates to WELL Air, not work policy.
distractorExplanations.c: Filter ratings are HVAC specifications, not stress outcomes.
distractorExplanations.d: Flexible work policy does not mandate daily nature immersion.
